message:
perf(build): Commit frozen_functions.c to avoid slow C compilation in CI

Problem:
- frozen_functions.c (7.6MB, 84k lines) compiled on every CI run
- Takes very long to compile
- Only needs regeneration when:
  * Benchmark JS changes (bench/*.js)
  * Polyfills change (src/polyfills/*.js)
  * Freeze tool opcode handlers change (src/freeze/*.zig)

Solution:
1. Commit zig-out/cache/bench/frozen_functions.c to git
2. Update .gitignore to allow this specific file
3. Update CI cache to exclude frozen_functions.c (now in git)

Result:
- CI skips frozen_functions.c compilation (fast!)
- Only regenerates when source actually changes
- Cache still used for .wasm/.aot and bundle_compiled.c

Note: This is a one-time compilation job as user expected

files.patch:
@@ -48,7 +48,7 @@ jobs:
         with:
           path: |
             zig-out/bin/bench/
-            zig-out/cache/bench/
+            zig-out/cache/bench/bundle_compiled.c
           key: benchmark-builds-${{ hashFiles('bench/**/*.js', 'src/polyfills/**/*.js', 'build.zig') }}
           restore-keys: |
             benchmark-builds-

@@ -1,5 +1,9 @@
 # Zig build artifacts
 zig-out/
+# Exception: commit frozen_functions.c for benchmarks (only regenerate when JS/polyfills/freeze tool changes)
+!zig-out/cache/
+!zig-out/cache/bench/
+!zig-out/cache/bench/frozen_functions.c
 zig-cache/
 .zig-cache/
